title: A001: Scaffold Go workspace and module structure
summary: Scaffolds Go workspace monorepo with go.work linking 3 modules (internal/common, operators/keystone, operators/c5c3), operator main.go entrypoints with controller-runtime manager setup (scheme registration, leader election, health probes, structured zap logging), top-level Makefile with 11 targets (working: build/test/lint/generate/manifests; stubs: docker-build/helm-package/e2e/deploy-infra/install-test-deps/test-integration), and .golangci.yml linter configuration. All modules compile, vet, and lint clean. GOWORK=off builds succeed via replace directives. go mod tidy is idempotent.

review_steps.1_INTENT: Plan specifies foundational Go workspace scaffold (S001) — base layer for all subsequent features. No business logic, no CRDs, no reconcilers.
review_steps.2_SCOPE: 20 files changed. All within scope: go.work, 3 go.mod, 2 main.go, Makefile, .golangci.yml, .gitignore, README.md, doc.go, 7 .gitkeep placeholders, 2 go.sum files, 1 progress JSON. No scope creep.
review_steps.3_VERIFICATION: go build: PASS (all 3 modules). go vet: PASS. golangci-lint run: PASS (0 violations). GOWORK=off go build: PASS (both operators). go mod tidy: idempotent (no changes). make build: PASS. make build OPERATOR=keystone: PASS. make test: PASS (no test files, expected). make lint: PASS. make generate/manifests: PASS (no-op echo). make docker-build: FAIL with expected $(error) message.
review_steps.4_PATTERNS: Both operator main.go files are structurally identical (differing only in package doc and LeaderElectionID). Both go.mod files are identical except module path. Consistent pattern established.
review_steps.5_TESTS: No test files — explicitly excluded per plan (S002). Scaffold phase has no business logic to test. main.go follows standard kubebuilder boilerplate.
review_steps.6_SECURITY: No user-facing inputs. No secrets, SQL, or path traversal. Operator binds to configurable addresses via flags. ctrl.GetConfigOrDie() is standard kubeconfig loading. No OWASP concerns.
review_steps.7_COMPLEXITY: Minimal implementation. Standard kubebuilder/controller-runtime patterns. No unnecessary abstractions.
review_steps.8_COMPLETENESS: All 12 tasks (1.1–4.1) marked done. All plan requirements verified: go.work, 3 go.mod, 2 main.go, Makefile (11 targets), .golangci.yml, .gitkeep dirs, .gitignore, README deviations documented.

tests_checklist.note: No tests — explicitly excluded (S002). Scaffold has no testable business logic.

code_quality_checklist.note: Both main.go files follow standard kubebuilder v4 / controller-runtime v0.23+ patterns.

code_quality_checklist.item: No code duplication beyond intentional operator symmetry

security_checklist.item: No user input validation needed (scaffold only)
Sensitive data not exposed in logs

architecture_checklist.item: Solution is as simple as possible

dry_yagni_checklist.item: No duplicated code (operator symmetry is intentional)

fail_fast_checklist.item: Stub targets fail explicitly with $(error) and feature reference
Health/ready probe setup errors cause os.Exit(1)

defensive_checklist.item: All external inputs validated (N/A for scaffold)
utilruntime.Must for scheme registration (panics on failure)

issues_found.description: Go version directive inconsistency: internal/common/go.mod uses `go 1.25` while go.work, operators/keystone/go.mod, and operators/c5c3/go.mod use `go 1.25.0`. Semantically identical but cosmetically inconsistent.
issues_found.fix: Run `cd internal/common && go mod tidy` with Go 1.25.x to normalize, or manually change to `go 1.25.0` for consistency. Note: the Go toolchain may normalize this differently depending on how the module was initialized.

issues_found.description: The build target outputs operator binaries to /dev/null (`go build -o /dev/null ./...`), which is correct for verification-only builds but differs from the common/module build on line 29 which has no -o flag. The asymmetry is technically correct (common has no main package) but could confuse future contributors.
issues_found.fix: Optional: add a brief comment on line 32 explaining why -o /dev/null is used (e.g., `# -o /dev/null: verify compilation only, no binary output in scaffold phase`).

suggested_improvements: Consider normalizing Go version directives across all go.mod files to the same format (1.25 vs 1.25.0) for cosmetic consistency.

The Makefile test target uses workspace-relative paths (go test ./internal/common/...) while build and lint targets use cd into each module. Both approaches work but a comment explaining the rationale would help future maintainers.
next_steps: S002: Add test infrastructure (envtest setup, Chainsaw E2E framework, install-test-deps target).
S003: GitHub Actions CI workflows for build/test/lint.
S004-S005: Shared library packages in internal/common/ (conditions, config, types).
S011: CRD type definitions in api/v1alpha1/ with controller-gen integration (will replace generate/manifests no-ops).

detected_patterns.name: Operator manager entrypoint structure
detected_patterns.applies_when: Creating a new operator module (e.g., operators/glance/, operators/nova/)
detected_patterns.description: Every operator main.go follows a fixed 6-step structure: (1) scheme registration in init() with clientgoscheme + kubebuilder scaffold marker, (2) flag parsing for metrics-bind-address, health-probe-bind-address, leader-elect, and zap options, (3) ctrl.NewManager with metricsserver.Options (not deprecated MetricsBindAddress string), (4) kubebuilder scaffold:builder marker for controller registration, (5) AddHealthzCheck + AddReadyzCheck with healthz.Ping, (6) mgr.Start(ctrl.SetupSignalHandler()). All errors cause setupLog.Error + os.Exit(1). LeaderElectionID follows the convention '<operator-name>.openstack.c5c3.io'.

examples.code:
func main() {
	var metricsAddr string
	var probeAddr string
	var enableLeaderElection bool

	flag.StringVar(&metricsAddr, "metrics-bind-address", ":8080", "The address the metric endpoint binds to.")
	flag.StringVar(&probeAddr, "health-probe-bind-address", ":8081", "The address the probe endpoint binds to.")
	flag.BoolVar(&enableLeaderElection, "leader-elect", false,
		"Enable leader election for controller manager, ensuring only one active controller manager.")
	opts := zap.Options{Development: true}
	opts.BindFlags(flag.CommandLine)
	flag.Parse()

	ctrl.SetLogger(zap.New(zap.UseFlagOptions(&opts)))

	mgr, err := ctrl.NewManager(ctrl.GetConfigOrDie(), ctrl.Options{
		Scheme: scheme,
		Metrics: metricsserver.Options{
			BindAddress: metricsAddr,
		},
		HealthProbeBindAddress: probeAddr,
		LeaderElection:         enableLeaderElection,
		LeaderElectionID:       "keystone.openstack.c5c3.io",
	})

func main() {
	var metricsAddr string
	var probeAddr string
	var enableLeaderElection bool

	flag.StringVar(&metricsAddr, "metrics-bind-address", ":8080", "The address the metric endpoint binds to.")
	flag.StringVar(&probeAddr, "health-probe-bind-address", ":8081", "The address the probe endpoint binds to.")
	flag.BoolVar(&enableLeaderElection, "leader-elect", false,
		"Enable leader election for controller manager, ensuring only one active controller manager.")
	opts := zap.Options{Development: true}
	opts.BindFlags(flag.CommandLine)
	flag.Parse()

	ctrl.SetLogger(zap.New(zap.UseFlagOptions(&opts)))

	mgr, err := ctrl.NewManager(ctrl.GetConfigOrDie(), ctrl.Options{
		Scheme: scheme,
		Metrics: metricsserver.Options{
			BindAddress: metricsAddr,
		},
		HealthProbeBindAddress: probeAddr,
		LeaderElection:         enableLeaderElection,
		LeaderElectionID:       "c5c3.openstack.c5c3.io",
	})

detected_patterns.name: Operator module dependency with local replace directive
detected_patterns.applies_when: Creating a new operator module that depends on internal/common
detected_patterns.description: Every operator go.mod requires internal/common at v0.0.0 (pseudo-version for local-only module) and declares a replace directive pointing to ../../internal/common for GOWORK=off CI builds. Direct dependencies are controller-runtime v0.23.x, apimachinery v0.35.x, and client-go v0.35.x. The module path follows github.com/c5c3/forge/operators/<name>.

examples.code:
module github.com/c5c3/forge/operators/keystone

go 1.25.0

require (
	github.com/c5c3/forge/internal/common v0.0.0
	k8s.io/apimachinery v0.35.1
	k8s.io/client-go v0.35.1
	sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime v0.23.1
)

// ... indirect deps ...

replace github.com/c5c3/forge/internal/common => ../../internal/common

module github.com/c5c3/forge/operators/c5c3

go 1.25.0

require (
	github.com/c5c3/forge/internal/common v0.0.0
	k8s.io/apimachinery v0.35.1
	k8s.io/client-go v0.35.1
	sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime v0.23.1
)

// ... indirect deps ...

replace github.com/c5c3/forge/internal/common => ../../internal/common
